title: Fractions of different wholes
description: Compare two fractions based on the whole that each one refers to.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jose went to a restaurant and ate $\dfrac13$ of the medium pizza.  

His sister Nina stayed home and ate $\dfrac13$ of a smaller pizza that their mother baked.

**Who ate more pizza?**  


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We don't know the size of $\red{\text{Nina's}}$ pizza, but we know it was *smaller* than $\green{\text{Jose's}}$ pizza.


$\qquad$

[[☃ image 1]]

$\qquad$

[[☃ image 2]]
Since $\red{\text{Nina's}}$ pizza was *smaller* than  $\green{\text{Jose's}}$ pizza, the $\dfrac13$ that $\red{\text{Nina}}$ ate is *less* than the $\dfrac13$ that $\green{\text{Jose's}}$ ate.
Jose ate more pizza.

choices.content: Jose
Nina
They ate the same amount.

assessment_items.question_content:
While the bears were out, Goldilocks ate all but $\dfrac38$ of Papa Bear's big green cake off his plate.

Then she ate all but $\dfrac38$ of the smaller pink cake on Baby Bear's plate.

**Whose plate had more cake left on it?**  


 
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\pink{\text{Baby Bear's}}$ cake was *smaller* than $\green{\text{Papa Bear's}}$ cake.

$\qquad$

[[☃ image 1]]

$\qquad$

[[☃ image 2]]



The amount of cake left on $\dfrac38$ of $\green{\text{Papa Bear's}}$ plate is *more* than the amount of cake left on $\dfrac38$ of $\pink{\text{Baby Bear's}}$ plate.
Papa Bear's plate had more cake left on it.

choices.content: Papa Bear's plate
Baby Bear's plate
Both plates had the same amount of cake left.

assessment_items.question_content:
A cow is $\dfrac13$ the height of a tree.  A horse is $\dfrac13$ the height of a taller tree.

**Who is taller?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We know the $\blue{\text{cow}}$ is $\dfrac13$ the height of this tree.  

[[☃ image 1]]

We don't know the height of the $\green{\text{horse}}$, but we know it is $\dfrac13$ the height of a *taller* tree.  

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]
The horse is taller.

choices.content: The cow
The horse
They both were the same height.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the video game Fantasy Farm, it takes $\dfrac56$ of a *minute* to harvest magic flowers, and it takes $\dfrac56$ of an *hour* to build a golden barn.

**Which takes more time?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Harvesting magic flowers}}$ takes $\dfrac56$ of a $\green{\text{minute}}$.

$\blue{\text{Building a golden barn}}$ takes $\dfrac56$ of an $\blue{\text{hour}}$.

We know that an $\blue{\text{hour}}$ is more than a $\green{\text{minute}}$, so $\dfrac56$ of an $\blue{\text{hour}}$ is more than $\dfrac56$ of a $\green{\text{minute}}$.
Building a golden barn takes more time.

choices.content: Harvesting magic flowers
Building a golden barn
They take the same amount of time.

assessment_items.question_content:
Luke had $\dfrac38$ of the glass of strawberry milkshake left to drink.  

Rita had $\dfrac38$ of a larger glass of mint milkshake left to drink.

**Who had more milkshake left to drink?**  


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\green{\text{Rita's}}$ glass was *larger* than $\pink{\text{Luke's}}$ glass. 

$\qquad$

[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]

There is *more* milkshake in $\dfrac38$ of a *larger* glass than in $\dfrac38$ of a smaller glass.
Rita had more milkshake left to drink.

choices.content: Luke
Rita
They had the same amount.

assessment_items.question_content:
Eli and Caleb painted two walls in a room, but they didn't finish the job. The walls were the same size.

Eli painted $\dfrac34$ of one wall.

Caleb painted $\dfrac34$ of the other wall.

**Who painted more area?**  



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\red{\text{Caleb}}$ painted a wall that was the same size as $\blue{\text{Eli's}}$ wall.

$\qquad$

[[☃ image 1]]

$\qquad$

[[☃ image 2]]
The $\dfrac34$ of the wall that $\blue{\text{Eli}}$ painted has the same area as the $\dfrac34$ of the wall that $\red{\text{Caleb}}$ painted.
Eli and Caleb painted the same area.

choices.content: Eli
Caleb
They painted the same amount.

assessment_items.question_content:
It took Hiro $\dfrac13$ of an hour to run from his house to school.

It took Fred $\dfrac13$ of an hour to run from his house to school.

**Who took more time running to school?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We know that Hiro ran for $\dfrac13$ of an hour.  


We know that Fred ran for $\dfrac13$ of an hour.
Since an hour is always the same amount of time, $\dfrac13$ of an hour is always the same amount of time, too.
Both activities took the same amount of time.

choices.content: Hiro
Fred
Both took the same amount of time.

assessment_items.question_content:
Katya is $\dfrac18$ the height of a tree in a forest. Olga is $\dfrac18$ the height of the same tree.

**Who is taller?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We know $\blue{\text{Katya}}$ is $\dfrac18$ the height of this tree.

$\qquad$

[[☃ image 1]]

We know $\green{\text{Olga}}$ is $\dfrac18$ the height of the same tree.

$\qquad$

[[☃ image 1]]

Katya and Olga are the same height.

choices.content: Katya
Olga
They are both the same height.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which pair of circles have equivalent areas shaded?**

However, for two fractions to be equivalent, they must refer to the same size whole.
Which answer choice shows $\dfrac13$ of the same size wholes shaded?

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which picture best helps us compare $\dfrac{3}{8}$ and $\dfrac{3}{6}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To compare fractions, both wholes must be the same size.

Which image shows $\dfrac36$ and $\dfrac38$ represented on the same size whole?

This image shows $\dfrac{3}{8}$ and $\dfrac{3}{6}$ on the same size wholes. Now it is fair to compare them!

[[☃ image 1]]

This image best allows us to compare the fractions.

[[☃ image 1]]

The second circle is small. It is divided into six equal parts. Three parts are shaded. The total shaded area of first circle covers more space than the shaded area of the second circle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2931af3f0587a881e73fe8b84023f55ce3e347eb)

The total shaded area of the first rectangle covers the same amount of space as the shaded area of the second rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/70ddc237955d9f0ff11ed08711154b51d2704fe0)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which picture best helps us compare $\dfrac{2}{4}$ and $\dfrac{1}{4}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To compare fractions, both wholes must be the same size.

Which image shows $\dfrac{2}{4}$ and $\dfrac{1}{4}$ represented on the same size whole?

This image shows $\dfrac{2}{4}$ and $\dfrac{1}{4}$ on the same size wholes. Now it is fair to compare them!

[[☃ image 1]]

This image best allows us to compare the fractions.

The total shaded area of triangle covers the same amount of space as the shaded area of the quadrilateral.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b0689505430f7f466b8738c0f71218c67bf73637)
